data.title: Charter stock falls despite revenue beat; Q2 2025: Revenue $13.77B (0.6% growth), EPS $9.18 - 117,000 internet customers lost.
data.description: Charter Communications (NASDAQ: CHTR) saw a decrease in its stock value following the release of its fiscal Q2 2025 results. The company revealed a 0.6% rise in quarterly revenue to $13.77 billion, surpassing the analyst consensus of $13.76 billion. The earnings per share stood at $9.18, falling short of the anticipated $9.80. Charter's revenue breakdown showed a 24.9% growth in residential mobile service, 2.8% in residential Internet, and 18.9% in other revenue. The company lost 117,000 internet customers compared to a 149,000 decline in Q2 of 2024, which included the impact of about 50,000 disconnects related to a previous event. Charter's potential deal with Cox poses a challenge to Comcast and could exceed AT&T in broadband services. More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Coursera's Q2 earnings exceeded expectations: EPS of 12 cents (vs. 9 cents expected), revenue of $187.1 million (vs. $180.48 million). CEO anticipates revenue of $738 to $746 million by 2025. Gained 7 million new learners.
data.description: Coursera, Inc. (NYSE: COUR) surpassed expectations with its second-quarter results announced after Thursday's market close. The company reported earnings of 12 cents per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of nine cents. Quarterly revenue totaled $187.1 million, exceeding the Street's estimate of $180.48 million. CEO Greg Hart highlighted the expanding market opportunity as global demand for acquiring new technology and skills rises. Coursera attracted over seven million new learners seeking to enhance their careers with emerging skills. The company raised its full-year 2025 revenue outlook by $17 million to a range of $738 million to $746 million. Coursera's shares rose by 1.3% at market close.

data.title: Global data center infrastructure market to reach $10.20 billion by 2031 with 16.1% CAGR, driven by data growth and IoT adoption.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, a recent extensive report from The Insight Partners revealed that the worldwide data center infrastructure market is experiencing notable advancement. This growth is attributed to the sharp rise in data generation and the increasing embrace of the Internet of Things (IoT). The market is expected to witness a substantial increase in revenue, with key factors such as rising demand for cloud services and digital transformations contributing to its positive trajectory. The report highlights the fast-paced developments in the industry, signaling promising opportunities for stakeholders.

data.title: Power Grid Components (PGC) appoints Michael Plaster as CEO, succeeding Rick McClure who will become Board Chairman.
data.description: In Bessemer, Alabama, Power Grid Components (PGC) has announced that Michael Plaster will become its new Chief Executive Officer starting August 18, 2025. With almost forty years of experience in electrical equipment and solutions firms, Michael is set to lead PGC, a major supplier of grid components in the country. He will take over from Rick McClure, who will move into the role of Chairman of the Board. This strategic transition marks a significant change in leadership within the company.

data.title: Eric Trump believes Ether's value should exceed $8,000, as global M2 money supply continues to rise.
data.description: A recent report revealed that the global economy is projected to shrink by 4.4% in 2020 due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. The report estimates that the global GDP will decrease by $700 billion, impacting various sectors and causing widespread economic uncertainty. The unemployment rate is expected to increase by 8.8%, equivalent to 300 million job losses worldwide. The report also highlights disparities in income and job losses, with vulnerable populations being disproportionately affected. Additionally, it predicts a slow recovery in 2021, with uncertainties surrounding the effectiveness of government interventions and the global response to the pandemic.

data.title: Quantum tech may recover lost Bitcoin by reverse-engineering private keys from public ones, threatening holders' security.
data.description: The potential advancement in quantum computing may lead to the decryption of private keys from their public counterparts. This poses a significant security threat to individuals holding Bitcoin, jeopardizing the safety of their assets.

data.title: Volcon, Inc. holds 3,183.37 BTC worth $375 million, averaging $117,697 per BTC. They authorized $100 million for share repurchase.
data.description: Volcon, Inc. (NASDAQ: VLCN), based in Austin, Texas, revealed today that they currently hold 3,183.37 BTC as of July 25th at 8:00am ET. This acquisition was made at an overall purchase cost of approximately $375 million, with an average price of $117,697 per BTC. Additionally, the company has engaged in the sale of several short-term put contracts. These contracts provide Volcon with the chance to acquire more BTC at $115,000, $116,000, and $117,000, while also receiving option premium.
